- Q2 (Q2-B+A): Verified by myself (A) and a dead player (B).
- Q3 (Q3-C+A): Verified by myself (A) and the other living player (C). This also has maximum consensus from all surviving players.
- Q4 (Q4-D+B+C): Verified by the other living player (C) and two dead players (D and B). While I (A) haven't personally verified this one, C's agreement provides strong confidence, especially considering the puzzles are designed to be solvable.

All four parts of the password have been verified by at least one of the two remaining players, and two parts (Q1, Q3) have been verified by both A and C. This level of consensus makes volunteering the most strategically sound decision to escape and preserve my remaining 2 coins, significantly mitigating the immediate and high risk of random death.

The only remaining puzzle that I have not personally verified is **Q2**. The only available capsule for it is **Q2-B+A**. This capsule is endorsed by A, an active player, who has proven to be reliable in previous games (A has won twice). Given the need to act to avoid random elimination, and the strong consensus on the other three puzzles, I will trust A's judgment on Q2.

This is the optimal time to make an attempt to escape with the maximum possible information, while mitigating the highest immediate risk of random elimination.
